LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR GROSS NEGLIGENCE. CALIFORNIA DOES NOT RECOGNIZE SUCH A CAUSE OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFF CAN RAISE THE ISSUE IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S RELEASE/WAIVER DEFENSE WITHOUT PLEADING THE MATTER IN THE COMPLAINT. SEE CITY OF SANTA BARBARA V. SUP. CRT. (2007) 41 CAL.4TH 747, 779-780; CONTINENTAL INS. CO. V. AMERICAN PROTECTION INDUSTRIES (1987) 197 CAL.APP.3RD 322. LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS KATHERINE LINDLEY AND KENNETH LINDLEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS BASED ON THE ALLEGATION THAT PLAINTIFF'S GRANDPARENTS HAVE SUED FOR GROSS NEGLIGENCE AND THERE CANNOT BE A CONTRACT WHICH WAIVES LIABILITY FOR FUTURE GROSS NEGLIGENCE. (SEE CITY OF SANTA BARBARA V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 41 CAL.4TH 747.) PLAINTIFF IS REQUESTING THE COURT TO FIND AT THIS STAGE THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS AMOUNTED TO GROSS NEGLIGENCE AND THAT THE WAIVER WAS THEREFORE, A CONTRACT OF ADHESION. SUCH DETERMINATIONS ARE NOT APPROPRIATE ON A DEMURRER BECAUSE A DEMURRER TESTS THE LEGAL SUFFICIENCY OF THE COMPLAINT AND DOES NOT WEIGH THE EVIDENCE OR FACTS PRESENTED. (SEE SISEMORE V. MASTER FINANCIAL INC. (2007) 151 CAL.APP.4TH 1386, 1397.) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE AT THE DEMURRER STAGE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT AMOUNTED TO GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR THAT THE WAIVER AMOUNTED TO AN AGREEMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S PART TO INDEMNIFY DEFENDANT FOR ITS ALLEGED GROSS NEGLIGENCE. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IMPLIED INDEMNITY AND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONTRIBUTION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT HAS ALLEGED FACTS THAT PLAINTIFF WAS LIABLE OR PARTLY LIABLE FOR MINOR O.C'S INJURIES. (SEE CROSS-COMPLAINT PARAGRAPH 28-31.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE RELEASE BARS "ANY AND ALL CLAIMS ARISING FROM THE USE" OF DEFENDANT'S FACILITY. MR. LINDLEY EXECUTED THAT RELEASE ON BEHALF OF ALL PLAINTIFFS. (KENNETH LINDLEY DEPO., 40:12-14; 43:10-25; 47:2-4; 49:19-50:16; KATHERINE LINDLEY DEPO., 136:5-18; 237:6-10.) MRS. LINDLEY'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BECAUSE ONE SPOUSE CAN BIND ANOTHER SPOUSE TO A RELEASE. (BUCKNER V. TAMARIN (2002) 98 CAL.APP.4TH 140, 142.) PLAINTIFF O.C.'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BECAUSE A PARENT OR GUARDIAN MAY EXECUTE A RELEASE ON BEHALF OF A MINOR. (AARIS V. LAS VIRGENES UNIFIED SCHOOL DIST. (1998) 64 CAL.APP.4TH 1112, 1120.) THE RELEASE IS CLEAR, UNAMBIGUOUS, AND EXPRESS AND THEREFORE EFFECTIVE. (BENNETT V. UNITED STATES CYCLING FEDERATION (1987) 193 CAL.APP.3D 1485, 1490.) PLAINTIFFS' INJURIES WERE WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE RELEASE AND ENFORCEMENT OF THE RELEASE DOES NOT VIOLATE PUBLIC POLICY. (KENNETH LINDLEY DEPO., 84:18-21; SHOSHANNA MOODY DEC., PAR. 13 AND ATTACHMENT B.) IN ADDITION, THE DOCTRINE OF PRIMARY ASSUMPTION OF THE RISK BARS PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. DEFENDANT OWED NO LEGAL DUTY TO PROTECT PLAINTIFF O.C. FROM THE PARTICULAR RISK OF INJURING HER ANKLE GIVEN THE NATURE OF THE RECREATIONAL ACTIVITY. THE DOCTRINE ALSO APPLIES TO BAR PLAINTIFF KATHERINE LINDLEY'S CLAIM FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (ERIKSSON V. NUNNINK (2015) 233 CAL.APP.4TH 708, 728.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant and cross-complainant House of Air, LLC's motion for summary adjudication is granted on its cross-claim for declaratory relief. Per the plain language of the parties' agreement, HOA is entitled to a defense and indemnity for plaintiffs' claims. The agreement does not violate public policy. The indemnity agreement is limited to HOA's ordinary negligence and vindicates the covenant not to sue, which is also limited to HOA's ordinary negligence. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate gross negligence that would render the defense and indemnity obligations unenforceable. Plaintiffs have not highlighted any evidence of alleged gross negligence that the court did not previously consider when it granted summary judgment in favor of HOA as to plaintiffs' complaint. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Cross-Complainant House Of Air, Llc For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, A. Cabral Bonner of Bonner & Bonner Law Offices I415-331-3070) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS COMPLAINANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC's Motion By Cross-Complainant House Of Air, Llc For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication is DENIED. House of Air concedes that Code of Civil Procedure 1008 requires "both newly discovered evidence and an explanation for the failure to have produced such evidence earlier," but says this requirement only applies to "final" orders, and not "interim" ones. (Mot. 4:28-5:8.) However, the 1992 opinion House of Air relies upon was superseded by a legislative amendment that expressly applies the requirement to interim orders. (Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 688.) And House of Air has not provided a satisfactory explanation for its failure to have produced evidence earlier. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Off Calendar Aug-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Attorney, Charles A. Bonner of Bonner & Bonner (415-331-3070) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Aternative Motion Summary Adjudication is DENIED. House of Air fails to carry its initial burden of production as to damages, which are a requisite element of the breach of contract and express indemnity counts at a minimum. House of Air merely says it "has incurred" attorneys' fees and costs without proving, or even stating, any amount or anything else regarding damages. (UMF 37.) Thus, summary judgment must be denied, and because the only issues of which House of Air seeks summary adjudication are its contract and express indemnity counts, summary adjudication must be denied too. (See CCP 437c(f)(1), (p)(1).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication, By Defendant House Of Air, Llc Request For Telephonic Appearance
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHARLES BONNER, ESQ. of Law Offices of Bonner and Bonner, Tel No. 415-331-3070 appearing for Plaintiffs;
Atty. DANIEL CROWLEY, ESQ. of Daniel Crowley & Associates, Tel No. 707-525-8999 appearing for Defendant House of Air, LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The release bars "any and all claims arising from the use" of defendant's facility. Mr. Lindley executed that release on behalf of all plaintiffs. (Kenneth Lindley Depo., 40:12-14; 43:10-25; 47:2-4; 49:19-50:16; Katherine Lindley Depo., 136:5-18; 237:6-10.) Mrs. Lindley's claims are barred because one spouse can bind another spouse to a release. (Buckner v. Tamarin (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 140, 142.) Plaintiff O.C.'s claims are barred because a parent or guardian may execute a release on behalf of a minor. (Aaris v. Las Virgenes Unified School Dist. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1112, 1120.) The release is clear, unambiguous, and express and therefore effective. (Bennett v. United States Cycling Federation (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 1485, 1490.) Plaintiffs' injuries were within the scope of the release and enforcement of the release does not violate public policy. (Kenneth Lindley Depo., 84:18-21; Shoshanna Moody Dec., par. 13 and attachment B.) In addition, the doctrine of primary assumption of the risk bars plaintiffs' claims. Defendant owed no legal duty to protect plaintiff O.C. from the particular risk of injuring her ankle given the nature of the recreational activity. The doctrine also applies to bar plaintiff Katherine Lindley's claim for emotional distress. (Eriksson v. Nunnink (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 708, 728.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)

Cross-Defendants Katherine Lindley And Kenneth Lindley'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-Defendants Katherine Lindley And Kenneth Lindley's Demurrer To 1st Amended Cross-Complaint is overruled. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. The demurrer is based on the allegation that Plaintiff's grandparents have sued for gross negligence and there cannot be a contract which waives liability for future gross negligence. (See City of Santa Barbara v. Superior Court (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747.) Plaintiff is requesting the court to find at this stage that Defendant's actions amounted to gross negligence and that the waiver was therefore, a contract of adhesion. Such determinations are not appropriate on a demurrer because a demurrer tests the legal sufficiency of the complaint and does not weigh the evidence or facts presented. (See Sisemore v. Master Financial Inc. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 1386, 1397.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for express indemnity is overruled because the court will not determine at the demurrer stage that Defendant's conduct amounted to gross negligence or that the waiver amounted to an agreement on Plaintiff's part to indemnify Defendant for its alleged gross negligence. The demurrer to the third cause of action for implied indemnity and the fourth cause of action for contribution is overruled. Defendant has alleged facts that Plaintiff was liable or partly liable for minor O.C's injuries. (See Cross-Complaint paragraph 28-31.) The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for gross negligence. California does not recognize such a cause of action and plaintiff can raise the issue in response to defendant's release/waiver defense without pleading the matter in the complaint. See City of Santa Barbara v. Sup. Crt. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 779-780; Continental Ins. Co. v. American Protection Industries (1987) 197 Cal.App.3rd 322. The court overrules the demurrer to the negligent hiring, supervision, etc. cause of action. See CCP 452. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H.
